summary: Iteration 153 - SMS Branch-Level Filtering testing. All 14 backend tests passed. All UI features verified working: branch filtering, multi-branch badges, blue/green bubble colors, auto-signature hint, thread header branch pill, sent_by_name display, and conversations reload on branch change.

minor.issue: Blast function uses 'business_info' key (line 474) while send function correctly uses 'company_info' (line 411). Settings DB only has 'company_info' key. Blast messages will fallback to 'AgriBooks' instead of actual company name in signatures.

action_items: Fix blast endpoint (line 474 in sms.py): change 'business_info' to 'company_info' and 'business_name' to 'name' to match what send_manual_sms uses
critical_code_review_comments: sms.py line 474: blast function uses db.settings.find_one({'key': 'business_info'}) but DB key is 'company_info'. Also uses .get('business_name') but field is 'name'. This causes blast messages to always show 'AgriBooks' as company name instead of the real name.

MessagesPage.js loadConversations() (line 132-137) does NOT explicitly pass branch_id - relies on axios interceptor in AuthContext.js. Works correctly because interceptor auto-appends branch_id for all GET requests when branch is selected, and useEffect re-runs on currentBranch change. Functionally correct but subtle.
The useEffect for conversations reload correctly depends on [activeTab, currentBranch] ensuring reload on branch change.
Branch filtering logic in backend correctly uses distinct phones + then fetches all outgoing messages for those phones (full thread for collaboration). Correct implementation.
Multi-branch badge rendering logic correct: shows colored badges only when branch_names.length > 1, uses green for current branch and blue for others.
Sent_by_name: correctly stored as user.get('full_name') or user.get('email', '') - if user has no full_name, falls back to email. Good.
seed_data_creation: Created test SMS messages via API: test phones 09123456789, 09123456799, 09111222333, 09222333444, 09333444555, 09444555666, 09333444555. Created multi-branch scenario: sent Branch 2 message to 09952568450 (JANMARK AHIG) which already had Branch 1 messages.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SMS branch-level filtering fully tested and working. All 9 features from the review request confirmed working. Minor issue: blast endpoint has wrong settings key ('business_info' vs 'company_info'). Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_sms_branch_filtering_153.py covers 14 test cases. Branch 1 ID: c435277f-9fc7-4d83-83e7-38be5b4423ac, Branch 2 ID: 18c02daa-bce0-45de-860a-70ccc6ed6c6d.

tests_performed.details: 15 conversations in All Branches view - all phones from all branches
7 conversations when Branch 1 selected - only phones Branch 1 has messaged

Conversations list has branch_ids and branch_names fields

Appends '\n\n- Sibugay Agricultural Supply | Branch 1' to message

tests_performed.test: POST /api/sms/send auto-appends company-only signature without branch
tests_performed.details: Appends '\n\n- Sibugay Agricultural Supply' when no branch_name
Stores 'Platform Admin' as sent_by_name for super admin user
Correct branch_id and branch_name persisted in sms_queue

tests_performed.test: GET /api/sms/conversation/{phone} outgoing messages have branch_id, branch_name, sent_by_name
tests_performed.details: All three fields present on outgoing messages in thread
Endpoint returns business_name field used by frontend for signature hint

tests_performed.test: UI: Conversations filtered when Branch 1 selected

mocked_apis.note: Physical SMS dispatch is MOCKED - messages are queued in sms_queue but not actually sent via SIM/Android gateway. All tests use queued/pending status.
